svg.text: The endpoint recurrence: one round’s move, repeated
Part 1 — how one selection round moves the value. Part 2 — that one move rolled forward from round 1 on three held-out runs.
Part 1 · How one round moves the current value
Mixed pool (some answers come from a supplier)
then a selection step of ρσ
Part 2 · Three held-out runs: observed vs the forecast rolled from round 1
endpoint error = mean-absolute error of the final-round value (0–1 scale); coverage = share of endpoints inside the 10–90% band. The two counts are different held-out sets — see caption.

Part 1 is a schematic (illustrative marks). Part 2 paths and band regenerated with stdlib from experiments/spread_util_unified.json first-round state via the committed unit recurrence and staged-noise residual pools.
Endpoint error read from experiments/unit_rollout_properties.json; coverage / CRPS read and asserted from experiments/trajectory_adjustment_bakeoff.json. Generator: model-endpoint-visual.py
